  • 当前位置：首页  师资力量  教职工概况  曹文翰  中文信息

曹文翰
助理教授、研究员、博导
博士毕业院校： 美国波士顿大学
电话： 021-20685102
办公室： 信息学院3-336
邮箱： whcao@shanghaitech.edu.cn
个人主页：
专业方向：
单位：
所属课题组：
研究方向： 柔性电子器件，软体机器人，太赫兹超表面器件，多层级微纳传感器件
招聘主页：
简介
团队
科研
教学
服务
成果
论文
影集
报道
主要岗位职责（A角）
兼任岗位职责（B角）
兼任岗位职业（C角）

曹文翰，博士，上海科技大学信息学院助理教授、研究员、博导。2015年于复旦大学信息学院获得本科学位，2020年于美国波士顿大学获得博士学位（导师：Prof. Keith A. Brown），2021年入选上海市海外高层次人才计划。曹文翰博士于2021年加入上海科技大学信息科学与技术学院后摩尔器件与集成系统中心，主持国家自然科学基金等多项国家级项目，并担任全国纳米技术标委会低维纳米委员会委员、中国光学工程学会高级会员、中国通信学会第一届太赫兹通信委员会委员、中国仿真学会集成微系统建模与仿真专委会第二届委员会委员。他的研究领域主要包括柔性电子器件、软体机器人、太赫兹超表面器件、多层级微纳传感器件等。研究论文以第一作者或通讯作者发表在Nature Communications、Nano Letters、PhotoniX、Optica、Advanced Science、Advanced Functional Materials、Laser & Photonics Reviews、Chemical Engineering Journal、Advanced Quantum Technologies、IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Quantum Electronics等学术期刊上，多篇论文入选科睿唯安ESI高被引论文检索。获得Wiley威立中国高贡献作者荣誉，担任FlexMat青年编委、Wearable Electronics青年编委、Soft Science青年编委、中国激光杂志社Chinese Optics Letters青年编委、《红外与毫米波学报》青年编委、《无线电通信技术》青年编委。担任Nature Communications、Matter、Laser & Photonics Reviews、Carbon、Photonics Research、Optics Express等期刊审稿人。授权美国专利1项，授权国家发明专利3项。

曹文翰课题组长期招收以下职位人员：（1）硕士、博士研究生，（2）博士后，（3）访问学生、学者，（4）本科生。课题组研究方向涉及高度交叉学科，申请人需有电子工程、通信工程、机械工程、材料学或化学等相关专业其中之一的知识背景。学校和学院将提供具有竞争力的工作条件和环境。请申请人将个人简历、成绩单和个人陈述发送至曹文翰博士的邮箱（whcao@shanghaitech.edu.cn）。

    毕业去向：

  1. 柔性电子器件


  2. 软体机器人


  3. 太赫兹超表面器件


  4. 多层级微纳传感器件

EE229《柔性电子学》

柔性电子学是一门新兴且具有广阔前景的交叉学科，其研究内容与应用场景涉及到信息技术、物理学、材料科学、生命科学、以及化学等领域。柔性电子学是通向高性能柔性传感器件、智能可穿戴设备以及智慧型医疗设备的重要途经，其研究具有重要的学术意义和应用价值。本课程通过讲授柔性电子器件的特性、基本结构组成、柔性材料的选择和制备技术，使学生获得柔性电子技术的基本理论、基本方法和基本技能。同时，了解柔性电子技术发展的概况，并初步掌握柔性电子的制造方法，为相关科学研究打下基础。


EE2211《微电子器件》

微电子器件是信息科学与技术领域的核心组成部分。从计算机处理器芯片到6G太赫兹通信，再到量子计算和集成光子芯片，微电子器件在其中都扮演着不可或缺的重要角色。微电子器件课程主要涵盖了微电子器件的发展历史、物理原理和设计思路，其中包括各类新型两端、三端微电子器件的介绍和研发现状和制备方法。同时，课程也会对其前沿发展方向如柔性微电子器件和集成光电子器件作详细的介绍。


1.        Ke Li, Yang Li, Haoran Wei, Jiangtao Tian, Jiaqi Zhang, Hongbo Wang, Zhiyu Dai, Yongjun Men, Wenhan Cao, and Zhongjie Huang. “Ultrasensitive piezoresistive microstructured sensors with controlled porosity for voiceprint recognition.” Chemical Engineering Journal 535 (2026): 175525.

2.        Yuhua Su, Binhe Wu, Chunrui Wang, Jian Zhou, Hao Sun, Wenhan Cao, and Huimei Yu. “Simultaneous differential scanning calorimetry–photoluminescence spectroscopy for Eu-doped LiYO2 phosphor with tunable phase transition.” Journal of Applied Physics 139.13 (2026): 133106.

3.        Xinyi Zhou, Haoran Wei, Qun Lang, Yuxin Lin, Tao Fang, and Wenhan Cao. “A fully stretchable octopus-inspired soft robotic tentacle empowered by multimodal tactile sensory toward embodied intelligence.” FlexMat 2.4 (2025): 591-607.

4.        Lingyun Zhu, Haoran Wei, Binhe Wu, and Wenhan Cao. “Terahertz holographic image encryption via thermally tunable directional Janus metasurface.” SmartMat 6.6 (2025): e70049.

5.        Ke Li, Hongbo Wang, Yang Li, Yunqing Bu, Chenyang Wang, Wenhan Cao, and Zhongjie Huang. “Advanced flexible hollow microstructures: fabrication strategies, multifunctional properties, and emerging applications.” Chemical Engineering Journal 525 (2025): 169855.

6.        Xiaoyong He, Wenhan Cao, and Fangting Lin. “Tunable BIC metamaterials with Dirac semimetals.” Nanophotonics 14.27 (2025): 4875-4925.

7.        Yunqing Bu, Taishan Yin, Haoran Wei, Jiaqi Zhang, Yue Huang, Wenhan Cao, and Zhongjie Huang. “Programmable 1D light–matter interaction: unlocking the optical potential of carbon nanotubes.” Advanced Optical Materials 13.30 (2025): e01957.

8.        Shilin Liu, Wenhan Cao, Jiaxin Li, and Xiaoyong He. “3D Dirac semimetal metamaterial supported terahertz tunable dual-functional polarization converter.” IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Quantum Electronics 31.5 (2025): 1-8.

9.        Wei Zhang, Sunyuan Zhang, Chunrui Wang, Binhe Wu, Jian Zhou, Hao Sun, Wenhan Cao. “Device modeling and numerical analysis of vo2-based oscillatory neuristors.” Journal of Applied Physics 137.20 (2025): 204501.

10.    Haoran Wei, Xiaoyong He, and Wenhan Cao. “Spin-multiplexed metasurface inverse-design based on a bi-directional deep neural network for terahertz wavefront control.” Optica 12.4 (2025): 505-517.

11.    Sunyuan Zhang, Binhe Wu, Chunrui Wang, Jian Zhou, Hao Sun, Wenhan Cao, and Huimei Yu. “Thermal evidences for multi-domain coexistence of freestanding vo2 crystalline microbeams with insulator–insulator transitions.” Applied Physics Letters 126.12 (2025): 121904.

12.    Zhaoyang Yu, Tao Fang, and Wenhan Cao. “Flexible and wearable devices based on colloidal quantum dots.” Advanced Materials Technologies 10.10 (2025): 2401983.

13.    Jiaqi Zhang, Tao Fang, Tingting Chen, Taishan Yin, Yuqing Zhao, Wenhan Cao, Huan Pang, and Zhongjie Huang. “Nature inspired phototropic artiﬁcial photosynthesis.” Advanced Functional Materials 35.22 (2025): 2422228.

14.    Tao Fang, Jiangtao Tian, and Wenhan Cao. “Highly stretchable carbon-based triboelectric nanogenerator textiles woven using coaxial fibres via direct ink writing.” Wearable Electronics 1 (2024): 195-204.

15.    Yuxin Lin, Xinyi Zhou, and Wenhan Cao. “3D-Printed Hydraulic Fluidic Logic Circuitry for Soft Robots.” arXiv preprint arXiv:2401.16827 (2024).

16.    Yuxin Lin, Jiaxuan Ma, Sizhe Gu, Jipeng Kong, Bowen Xu, Xiting Zhao, Dengji Zhao, Wenhan Cao, and Sören Schwertfeger. “Collecting large-scale robotic datasets on a high-speed mobile platform.” 2024 IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Biomimetics (ROBIO). IEEE, 2024.

17.    Jiahao Wang, Binhe Wu, Chunrui Wang, Jian Zhou, Hao Sun, Wenhan Cao, and Huimei Yu. “Relieving photobleaching impacts on fluorescence thermometry via neural network predictions.” Applied Optics 63.30 (2024): 7857-7864.

18.    Shaopeng Li, Jiangtao Tian, Ke Li, Kemeng Xu, Jiaqi Zhang, Tingting Chen, Yang Li, Hongbo Wang, Qiye Wu, Jinchun Xie, Yongjun Men, Weiping Liu, Xiaodan Zhang, Wenhan Cao, and Zhongjie Huang. “Intelligent song recognition via a hollow-microstructure-based, ultrasensitive artificial eardrum.” Advanced Science 11.42 (2024): 2405501.

19.    Yan Cheng, Wenhan Cao, and Xiaoyong He. “Hybrid plasmonic waveguides with tunable ENZ phenomenon supported by 3D Dirac semimetals.” Laser & Photonics Reviews 18.9 (2024): 2400167.

20.    Jiangtao Tian and Wenhan Cao. “Reconfigurable flexible metasurfaces: from fundamentals towards biomedical applications.” PhotoniX 5.1 (2024): 2.

21.    Aiqiang Nie, Xiaoyong He, and Wenhan Cao. “Carbon-based ultrabroadband tunable terahertz metasurface absorber.” Advanced Photonics Nexus 3.1 (2024): 016007.

22.    Shilin Liu, Wenhan Cao, Shizeng Jiang, Lianhao He, Fangting Lin, and Xiaoyong He. “3D Dirac semimetal supported tunable multi-frequency terahertz metamaterial absorbers.” Advanced Quantum Technologies 7.4 (2024): 2300386.

23.    Sen Lin, Binhe Wu, Chunrui Wang, Jian Zhou, Hao Sun, and Wenhan Cao. “Optimized design of nano-photonic devices for temperature self-regulating on vanadium dioxide thin films.” Journal of Optics 26.2 (2024): 025101.

24.    Guangqing Wang, Wenhan Cao, and Xiaoyong He. “3D Dirac semimetal elliptical fiber supported THz tunable hybrid plasmonic waveguides.” IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Quantum Electronics 29.5 (2023): 1-7.

25.    Xiaoyong He and Wenhan Cao. “Tunable terahertz hybrid metamaterials supported by 3D Dirac semimetals.” Optical Materials Express 13.2 (2023): 413-422.

26.    Yan Cheng, Wenhan Cao, Guangqing Wang, Xiaoyong He, Fangting Lin, and Feng Liu. “3D Dirac semimetal supported thermal tunable terahertz hybrid plasmonic waveguides.” Optics Express 31.11 (2023): 17201-17214.

27.    Xinyi Zhou and Wenhan Cao. “Flexible and stretchable carbon-based sensors and actuators for soft robots.” Nanomaterials 13.2 (2023): 316.

28.    Abigail Rendos, Wenhan Cao, Margaret Chern, Marco Lauricella, Sauro Succi, Jörg Werner, Allison Dennis, and Keith A. Brown. “Electric field induced macroscopic cellular phase of nanoparticles.” Soft Matter 18.10 (2022): 1991-1996.

29.    Wenhan Cao, Nourin Alsharif, Zhongjie Huang, Alice White, YuHuang Wang, and Keith A. Brown. “Massively parallel cantilever-free atomic force microscopy.” Nature Communications 12.1 (2021): 1-7.

30.    Wenhan Cao and Keith A. Brown. “Theory for hierarchical assembly with dielectrophoresis and the role of particle anisotropy.” Electrophoresis 42.5 (2021): 635-643.

31.    Wenhan Cao, Margaret Chern, Allison M. Dennis, and Keith A. Brown. “Measuring nanoparticle polarizability using fluorescence microscopy.” Nano Letters 19.8 (2019): 5762-5768.

32.    Wenhan Cao. “Analytical formulas for carrier density and Fermi energy in semiconductors with a tight-binding band.” Journal of Semiconductors 36.4 (2015): 042002.

33.    Wenhan Cao and Yee Sin Ang. “Effect of asymmetry on nonlinear optical response in graphene.” Europhysics Letters 107.3 (2014): 37007.
